# Extracao: transcription_mp4_05-Hiring_a_Virtual_Acquisition_Assistant

- **Fonte:** transcription_mp4_05-Hiring_a_Virtual_Acquisition_Assistant.txt
- **Tipo:** TXT
- **Tamanho:** 0 MB

---

Before we dig in, depending on your situation, you may want to think about hiring a virtual acquisition assistant to help you execute on this process. Later on, we'll discuss how to duplicate yourself in the business you acquire so you can free up your time while generating cash flow. But you can also think about the business acquisition process we'll cover here as a business you're running, where you can duplicate yourself and thereby free up a lot of your time. There are a lot of processes like generating business acquisition leads, researching their industry and business, sending emails or physical mail, coordinating with your deal team, organizing files and tracking items down and negotiating certain aspects of the business, and that's all stuff that we'll be covering. And after doing them yourself and documenting the process, you can leverage a virtual assistant to help you with different tasks. First, you can do the process yourself. And while you do, document your processes. Keep track of your templates and the desired outcomes. Then bring on a virtual assistant and train them while they watch you go through the process and can ask you questions. Then pass off the process to them and manage them. Hiring a virtual acquisition assistant absolves you from the headaches and extra money and benefits, taxes, insurance, overhead, all the costs associated with hiring an in-house employee. Their hours can expand or contract each week depending on what you need done, too. We'll include a few resources here for tasks that VA's can handle for you and how to interview them to make sure you're not wasting your time with a poor candidate. At this stage, you should not go hire a virtual assistant. You should go through this course once or twice and thoroughly understand everything involved and that you're going to be working on so that you understand the process yourself. But keep this lesson in mind for your ongoing acquisition work because it will save you a lot of time and help you automate the process.
